For IT/OT Security Leaders

Zero Network Footprint. Full Asset Visibility.

SAM4 monitors rotating assets from the motor control cabinet. No OT network connection. No sensors on machines. No attack surface expansion.

Download the Security Architecture Brief Schedule a Security Review

Condition Monitoring Usually Means Network Risk

Most condition monitoring systems require OT network access, wireless sensor gateways, cloud connectivity from the plant floor, and ongoing firmware updates. Each creates an attack vector. IT/OT teams push back, rightly, and projects stall.

The Architecture Problem

Traditional Monitoring

Requires wireless gateways on OT side. Real-time cloud sync. Firmware patches pushed to plant network.

SAM4 Approach

Current/voltage sensors connect only to MCC. Edge device stays local. Outbound IT network only.

No Attack Surface

Separated from SCADA, DCS, PLCs. No inbound connections. No remote shell access.

How SAM4 Connects and What It Doesn't Touch

1

Sensors

Current and voltage sensors clip onto power cables at the motor control cabinet (MCC).

2

Edge Processing

Hardened Linux device processes signals locally. No cloud dependency for monitoring or diagnostics.

3

IT Network Only

Data transmitted via standard HTTPS outbound only (port 443). No connection to OT network, PLCs, SCADA, or DCS.

4

Physical Separation

SAM4 sits on the electrical supply side, physically isolated from OT control systems.

Security by Architecture, Not Just Policy

Area Specification
Network Outbound HTTPS only (port 443). No inbound connections. No OT network access. Zero trust architecture.
Data Transmitted Current and voltage waveforms only. No process data, no PII, no production secrets, no configuration files.
Authentication SSO/SAML support. Multi-factor authentication. Role-based access control (RBAC). API token-based auth.
Infrastructure AWS hosted (EU region available). SOC 2 Type II certified. ISO 27001 certified. NIS2 aligned.
Edge Device Hardened Linux. No remote shell access. Automatic security updates. Regular penetration testing.
Data Residency EU or US hosting. Customer choice. GDPR compliant. No third-party data transfer.

Questions We Hear From Every CISO

Does SAM4 need OT network access?

No. It connects to the IT network only. Outbound HTTPS. There is no OT-side component that requires access to the control network.

What data leaves the plant?

Current and voltage waveforms only. No process variables, no PII, no alarm histories, no configuration data. The electrical signature is anonymized before transmission.

Can we host on-premises?

Not currently. SAM4 is cloud-hosted on AWS. We offer EU data residency for compliance. Private cloud options are under evaluation.

What happens if the connection drops?

The edge device buffers data locally. Monitoring continues without interruption. Data syncs automatically when the connection restores. No loss of detection capability.

How do you handle vulnerability management?

Regular penetration testing by third-party firms. Automated patching on edge devices. Responsible disclosure programme. Vulnerability assessment reports available on request.

Is there an API?

Yes. RESTful API with token-based authentication. CMMS and EAM integration. Webhook support for custom workflows. Full API documentation provided.

Built for Regulated Environments

SOC 2 Type II

Security, availability, and confidentiality controls independently audited and validated.

ISO 27001

Information security management system certified. Continuous compliance monitoring.

GDPR Compliant

Data processing agreements in place. Residency options. Right to deletion and portability.

NIS2 Aligned

Critical infrastructure framework. Operates in water utilities, pharmaceuticals, and oil and gas.

Real-world deployment: SAM4 operates in NIS2 critical infrastructure (water utilities), GxP-regulated environments (pharmaceuticals), and ATEX zones (oil and gas). Security review documentation and architecture diagrams available on request. Customer due diligence packages available for your procurement process.

Ready to Review the Architecture?

We'll share our network architecture diagram, data flow documentation, penetration test summary, and API specifications for your security review.

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
